commit.message:
Reload file in vim after Write tool to trigger filetype detection

Previously the analyze hook only reloaded files when a diff preview was
active (Edit tool). Write tool changes bypassed the reload, leaving vim
without proper filetype detection. Now the hook always enters the edit
branch and falls back to a simple `:e! | filetype detect` when no diff
preview temp file exists.

commit.message:
Fix admin login: establish browser session after magic link auth

Root cause: the implicit-flow callback only sent the token to the Chrome
extension — it never set a browser cookie. The admin page auth check
(createServerClient().auth.getUser()) reads cookies, so it always saw an
unauthenticated user and looped back to sign-in.

Fix — four changes:

1. app/api/auth/set-session/route.ts (NEW)
   POST endpoint that receives { access_token, refresh_token } and calls
   supabase.auth.setSession() via the SSR client, writing auth cookies onto
   the response. The browser applies them, making subsequent server-rendered
   page visits see an authenticated user.

2. app/auth/callback/route.ts
   - Step 2 now POSTs to /api/auth/set-session (credentials: include) before
     doing anything else, so every sign-in flow sets cookies regardless of
     whether the extension is present.
   - Reads ?next= query param; if present, redirects there after auth (e.g.
     back to /admin). Falls back to /dashboard if no next and no extension.
   - Fixes misleading copy: 'Activating Katana in your browser…' → 'Verifying
     your sign-in link…'

3. app/auth/signin/page.tsx
   - Reads ?next= param and passes it in emailRedirectTo so the magic link
     carries the destination through: /auth/callback?next=/admin#access_token=…
   - Fixes sent-state copy: removes 'the Katana extension activates
     automatically' (wrong for web-only sign-ins like admin login).

4. app/admin/page.tsx
   - redirect('/auth/signin') → redirect('/auth/signin?next=/admin') so the
     full round-trip lands back on the admin page after sign-in.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add target generation and per-feature scaling pipelines

Implements the bridge between feature generation and model training:
- src/targets/: PIPs algorithm, regime labels (5-class from PIP segments),
  reconstruction target selection, forward prediction targets (vol, momentum, volume)
- src/scaling/: RobustScaler and StandardScaler dataclasses, fit on training
  data only, pickle-serializable for deployment
- src/utils/io.py: shared helpers (safe_filename, find_latest_snapshot, load_feature_parquets)
- CLI scripts (generate_targets.py, fit_scalers.py) and run.sh subcommands
- Config additions: targets and scaling sections in training.yaml

commit.message:
Add comprehensive interaction logging to Supabase

New edge function (log-interaction) and database table (interaction_logs)
capture every user action in the qualification flow: popup open/close,
type selection, domain submission, verification results, OTP events,
form submission, category toggles, product clicks, and call requests.

Uses navigator.sendBeacon for fire-and-forget, non-blocking logging.
Each event includes session_id, IP, user agent, referrer, and timestamp.

commit.message:
fix: add async: true to SessionEnd hook to prevent upload cancellation

Race condition confirmed: 6/150 sessions (4%) silently fail when Claude Code
kills the hook subprocess before completion. Async detaches from parent
signal group so the upload survives session teardown.

Refs DaveX2001/deliverable-tracking#1096

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: Enable TLS unit tests in CI by setting ARROW_TEST_DATA and regenerating keystores

The TLS tests (ConnectionTlsTest, ConnectionTlsRootCertsTest,
ConnectionMutualTlsTest) were failing because ARROW_TEST_DATA was not
set and the JKS keystores were stale. CI now generates keystores from
the arrow-testing submodule certs and sets ARROW_TEST_DATA.

Also treats UNAUTHENTICATED as benign during CloseSession, since
unauthenticated connections cannot close server-side sessions.

commit.message:
chore(sdk-python): update dependencies

- pydantic >=2.11.2 → >=2.12.5
- jsonpath-python >=1.0.6 → >=1.1.4
- pytest >=8.0.0 → >=9.0.2 (Python >=3.10)
- pytest-asyncio >=0.24.0 → >=1.3.0 (Python >=3.10)

pytest 9+ and pytest-asyncio 1.3+ require Python >=3.10, so version
markers are added to keep Python 3.9 compatibility with older versions.

commit.message:
Fix persistent pycares thread by using ThreadedResolver in test fixture

The _run_safe_shutdown_loop thread was spawned by pycares (via aiodns)
when TCPConnector created its default DNS resolver, not by aiohttp's
connector cleanup. This thread never exits, so no sleep/poll could fix
it. Switch to ThreadedResolver (stdlib getaddrinfo) since aioresponses
mocks all HTTP calls and the resolver is never used.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
